member.name: Dame Nia Griffith
member.synopsis: Dame Nia Griffith is the Labour MP for Llanelli , and has been an MP continually since 5 May 2005.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Wind Power: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the latest allocation of contracts for off-shore wind electricity generation on domestic energy security.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the CfD AR7 offshore wind auction, we secured more OFW capacity than any offshore wind CfD round to date, with 8.4GW of new capacity. That supports our energy security by powering the equivalent of over 12 million homes, while protecting the country fr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps he is taking to support disabled people into work.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to championing the rights of, and helping, disabled people and those with long-term health conditions. Last week we announced the expansion of our groundbreaking programme WorkWell across England, up to 250,000 more people wit...

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, When he plans to publish a steel strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I understand this is a worrying time for Tata’s staff and suppliers and I am in contact with Tata regarding their extended maintenance periods at Trostre, Port Talbot Hot Rolled Products and Llanwern Pickle-line sites. Tata staff affected will be paid at ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Scotland Office: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Scotland, what steps he has taken to reduce the cost of fraud in his Department in the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Scotland Office reported no cases of fraud or amounts lost due to fraud or error in any of the last three financial years. This is disclosed as part of the Annual Report and Accounts available on GOV.UK.The Government is determined to uncover fraud in...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Justice: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps he has taken to reduce the cost of fraud in his Department in the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice (MoJ) recognises that the nature of the fraud threat it faces is constantly evolving and that our response must be equally as agile. The MoJ has been developing its Counter Fraud Centre of Expertise (CoEx) since 2019 and this team ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Northern Ireland Office: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, what steps he has taken to reduce the cost of fraud in his Department in the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Northern Ireland Office reported no cases of fraud or amounts lost due to fraud or error in any of the last three financial years. This is disclosed as part of the Annual Report and Accounts available on GOV.UK. The Government is determined to uncover...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Justice: Domestic Visits
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what the destinations were of domestic overnight visits undertaken by Ministers within their Department in each of the last three financial years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to my response to PQ 17532 on 13 March 2024.

Name of donor: UK Friends of Ukraine Ltd Address of donor: 81 High Street, Cosham, Portsmouth, England, PO6 3BL Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): £768.04 conference (£372.04), Transfers (minbus) (£262.12), BUCC-USUBC Roundtable lunch (£45.88), dinner and performance by Cultural Forces (£28.00), visit to Vinnytsia, transfers (£8.00), lunch (£18.00) and dinner (£34.00) Destination of visit: Ukraine (Kyiv and Vinnytsia) Dates of visit: 10 September 2025 to 15 September 2025 Purpose of visit: To attend the Yalta European Strategy conference, meet the Speaker and MPs from the Ukrainian Parliament and have meetings with representatives of businesses, civic organisations and public bodies.
